commit.message:
fix: await getBurner before modal close to unblock navigation

getBurner() sets delegatorAddress which triggers ConnectWalletModal's
navigation effect. Previous ordering called onClose() first, setting
isOpen=false — the effect short-circuited and navigation never fired.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update ramp dashboard avg replies calculation to match cohort view

Fix avg_replies_per_merchant in ramp dashboard to use same calculation
as cohort performance view:

Before (wrong):
  AI replies sent / All merchants = 3 / 20 = 0.15

After (correct):
  Inbound emails / Merchants who responded = 3 / 2 = 1.5

This ensures consistency across all dashboard views and shows the
correct average replies from responding merchants.

commit.message:
Rewrite matchmaking with onSnapshot, tiered search, and tolerance

Root cause of players not matching: polling with getDocs was unreliable
and the fire-and-forget pool write silently failed. Players never saw
each other in the pool.

Fixes:
- Use onSnapshot on matchmaking pool for instant real-time detection
  instead of unreliable setInterval polling
- Await the initial pool write and show error if it fails
- Changed pool path from nested public/data/matchmaking_pool to
  simpler artifacts/{appId}/matchmaking_pool for cleaner access
- Use setTimeout for 30s AI fallback instead of checking inside poll

New features:
- Scanning tolerance: starts at 0% win-rate difference, increases
  10% every 2 seconds during the 30-second search window
- Tiered search: among matchable opponents, picks the one with
  the closest totalGames count (veterans vs beginners)
- UI shows current tolerance percentage in real-time
- Fallback to AI after 30 seconds if no human found

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Update Transformation price to 549€, change badge icon, add 1€ test card

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat(auth): OAuth 2.1 proxy AS with SDK v1.27 (#5)

* feat(auth): add OAuth 2.1 proxy AS with SDK v1.27 auth router

Replace custom auth middleware with SDK's mcpAuthRouter, which provides
all standard OAuth 2.1 endpoints (AS metadata, authorize, token, register,
revoke) plus RFC 9728 protected resource metadata.

The server now acts as a proxy Authorization Server that delegates user
authentication to the Elnora platform login page and issues its own
MCP access tokens — never passing through the client's token (CoSAI MCP-T1).

Key changes:
- Upgrade @modelcontextprotocol/sdk from ^1.12.0 to ^1.27.0
  (fixes CVE GHSA-345p-7cg4-v4c7)
- Add ElnoraOAuthProvider implementing OAuthServerProvider interface
- Add InMemoryClientsStore for Dynamic Client Registration (RFC 7591)
- PKCE S256 mandatory (handled by SDK)
- Short-lived access tokens (1h) with refresh token rotation (CoSAI MCP-T7)
- Audience binding via RFC 8707 resource parameter (CoSAI MCP-T9)
- Platform OAuth callback route for auth code relay
- New env vars: ELNORA_PUBLIC_URL, ELNORA_LOGIN_URL,
  ELNORA_TOKEN_EXCHANGE_URL, ELNORA_PLATFORM_CLIENT_ID,
  ELNORA_PLATFORM_CLIENT_SECRET

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* feat(security): add CoSAI security hardening (CORS, scopes, logging, rate limits, SBOM)

Implements 5 CoSAI MCP security controls:

1. CORS + CSRF (MCP-T7): Restrictive CORS policy — only allows
   configured origins (MCP server + platform). Blocks cross-origin
   attacks. CSRF on /oauth/callback via mcp_code session validation.

2. Tool invocation logging (MCP-T12): Structured JSON audit log for
   every tool call — tool name, sanitized params, client ID, duration,
   success/failure. Content fields redacted to avoid PII in logs.

3. Per-tool scope enforcement (MCP-T2): Each tool declares required
   scopes (e.g. elnora_upload_file needs files:write). Insufficient
   scopes return an error before the tool executes. Least privilege.

4. Rate limiting on /mcp (MCP-T10): 60 req/min per client with
   standard X-RateLimit-* headers and Retry-After on 429.

5. SBOM generation (MCP-T11): CI pipeline generates CycloneDX SBOM
   on every build, uploaded as artifact for supply chain visibility.

Also: payload size limit (1MB) on express.json() body parser.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Fix slideshow stuck after ended video when new media uploaded

When a single long video finished playing and new media was added via
WebSocket, the auto-advance effect would set waitingForVideo=true again
but the video had already ended, so onEnded would never fire — leaving
the slideshow stuck. Now detects videoRef.ended and advances immediately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Drop essentia for beat tracking, use librosa with BPM hint

Evaluated librosa vs essentia across 8 tracks (120/130/148 BPM).
Librosa with known BPM hint is reliable; without hints it fails
(e.g. Blue Monday detected at 86 BPM half-time). Essentia requires
numpy<2 which conflicts with PyTorch — removing it unblocks the
cloud pipeline.

- Remove essentia import from phrase_detect.py
- Use librosa.beat.beat_track with required --bpm argument
- Add eval_beat_tracking.py evaluation script and results JSON

Closes #8

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: group merchants by email only, not by merchant_id

Remove merchant_key from GROUP BY clause to properly group merchants
by normalized email address only. This prevents merchants from appearing
as duplicate rows when merchant_id differs between outreach and inbound emails.

Before: GROUP BY merchant_key, merchant_email_normalized, cohort...
  - Outreach has merchant_id = "003UW..."
  - Inbound has merchant_id = NULL or different
  - Result: 2 separate rows for same merchant

After: GROUP BY merchant_email_normalized, cohort...
  - Groups all emails for same merchant together
  - Uses MAX(merchant_key) to select one merchant_id
  - Result: 1 row per merchant

This fixes the issue where 20 merchants appeared as 22 rows.

Add CPU-only test_build.py that runs inside the built image before ECR
push — validates imports, versions, file presence, and Vulkan ICD. If
tests fail, the build aborts and does not push to ECR. Results are
uploaded to S3 for review.

Also add cloud/ecs/test.sh + cmd_test() in run.sh for on-demand GPU
test execution via ECS (runs test_environment.py + test_functional.py).

- Model.cpp: Add bounds to sscanf format strings
- LowLevelWindow_SDL.cpp: Replace strcpy/strcat/putenv with setenv()

Path traversal fixes:

fix: correct email extraction using SUBSTRING instead of REGEXP_REPLACE

Replace REGEXP_REPLACE with SUBSTRING for email normalization to fix
issue where inbound emails were returning control characters instead
of actual email addresses.

Before: REGEXP_REPLACE returning '\u0001' for "Name <email>" format
After: SUBSTRING FROM '<([^>]+)>' properly extracts email address

This fixes TruGlow appearing as 2 rows (one with blank email) and
ensures all merchants group correctly by normalized email.

fix(checkout): don't throw when Karma wallet not configured

getCardDetails(user.karma.skAgent) was throwing KARMA_CARD_DETAILS_ERROR
when skAgent was null (no wallet connected). Because approve_order is
fire-and-forget, the error was swallowed and the user saw "Order
submitted!" while the browser never actually launched.

Fix: fall back to the test Visa card when no Karma wallet is present,
same as DRY_RUN / DEMO_MODE. Logs a warning so it's visible in Railway.
Browser now fires regardless of wallet status.

commit.message:
feat(optimizer): CMA-ES sampler, active pruning, stricter trade floor

- Switch from TPE to CMA-ES sampler (with TPE warmup) for faster
  convergence in 14-param continuous search space
- Add active pruning: trial.report() + should_prune() after each AWF
  split, with reduction_factor=2 (prune 50% of weak trials early)
- Raise MIN_TRADES_YEAR_HARD from 30 to 60 to filter low-sample pairs
- Remove soft trade ramp (TARGET_TRADES_YEAR) — hard constraint only
- Save best_trial_number in stage1 results for convergence analysis
- Cap n_jobs at 3 per pair for better thread utilization
- Add cmaes>=0.12.0 dependency

commit.message:
Fix relay rotation bugs found during audit

- Serialize OnActive/OnIdle with activeMu to prevent races on FFmpeg
  state and relay active counts (could permanently leak connections)
- Loop on isActive() after consuming wakeCh to discard stale signals
  that caused spurious connect-disconnect-reconnect cycles
- Acquire activeMu in shutdown goroutine so relay cleanup reads the
  final relay after any in-flight rotation completes (prevents fd leak)
- Skip detach/attach when rotateRelay picks the same stream URL
  (avoids needless audio gap)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Move recording preview to separate popup window (fullscreen)

The preview was showing inside the recording itself (Inception).
Now it opens in a separate window.open() popup that can be
dragged to a second monitor. Opens near-fullscreen sized.

Controls in the popup header:
- Size (S/M/L): adjust webcam overlay size in the recording
- Position (TL/TR/BL/BR): move webcam to any corner

Canvas mirrors at 5fps to keep CPU low.
Popup auto-closes when recording stops.
